data.title: Schneider Electric extends revenue growth forecast to 7% to 10%
data.description:
Schneider Electric (SBGSF) (SBGSY) raised its medium-term outlook on Thursday and outlined a substantial share repurchase plan that could reach $4.09 billion by the end of the decade.

During its capital markets day, the French industrial group said it now expects annual organic revenue growth of 7% to 10% from 2026 through 2030, effectively extending its current guidance. The company also projects that its adjusted ebitda margin will improve by a cumulative 250 basis points over the same period, with cash conversion holding near 100%.

Schneider Electric (SBGSF) (SBGSY) additionally announced a planned share buyback program of €2.5 billion to €3.5 billion ($2.92 billion to $4.09 billion) running through 2030, alongside a targeted divestment effort involving €1 billion to €1.5 billion in revenue to be completed by the end of the decade.

data.title: Atossa gains on Rare Pediatric Disease status for Duchenne asset
data.description:
• Atossa Therapeutics (ATOS) is up ~11% in Thursday trading after reporting that the US FDA has granted Rare Pediatric Disease designation to Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy candidate "Z"-Endoxifen.
  • If "Z"-Endoxifen is eventually approved, Atossa would be eligible for a Priority Review voucher that could be sold to another company.
  • "Z"-Endoxifen is a selective estrogen receptor modulator that Atossa says has 100 times greater potency than existing SERMs with better tolerability. It is also under investigation for breast cancer.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: MSFT, SMCI, CBRL, NFLX
data.description:
Recent analyst actions have highlighted significant updates in market sentiment, with changes in ratings for key companies like Microsoft (MSFT) and Netflix (NFLX). Analysts have upgraded Microsoft, acknowledging its strategic repositioning in underperforming segments, and Netflix for making bold acquisition moves that promise to strengthen its competitive edge. On the other hand, recent downgrades have affected companies like Super Micro Computer (SMCI) and Cracker Barrel (CBRL) due to concerns over competitive pressures and brand challenges.

Upgrades

  • Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Florian Muller. Despite some underperforming segments, Microsoft demonstrated strong free cash flow conversion and a narrowing valuation premium, justifying the upgrade.

    “MSFT impressively continues to grow free cash flow despite some of the boldest datacenter capex outflows on earth, maintaining robust FCF conversion. This deserves a premium, which has even narrowed compared to earlier this year.”

  • Netflix, Inc. (NFLX): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Multiplo Invest. The bold bid to acquire Warner Bros. is expected to enhance Netflix’s competitive advantages by combining its massive subscriber base with premier Hollywood intellectual property.

    “The acquisition would be incredible, in my opinion. Netflix would simply combine the strength of its massive subscriber base with the most well-regarded content in Hollywood … Netflix managers believe that the future is not just in streaming, but in physical recording and content production assets.”

Downgrades

  • Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI): Downgrade Hold to Sell by Tangerine Tan Capital. With declining ROIC and profit margins, SMCI faces intense pressure from competition, particularly noting inefficiencies in capital allocation as a significant concern.

    “Super Micro has no competitive advantage, and I don’t see any multiple or margin expansion potential. … I am pessimistic about Super Micro’s future, I must downgrade my rating to sell.”

  • Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. (CBRL): Downgrade Neutral to Sell by Gary Alexander. The company struggles with significant brand and customer base issues, compounded by a high leverage ratio, weakening its financial standing.

    “Cracker Barrel is increasingly looking like a value trap as its core customer base flees. … With expected double-digit traffic declines in Q2 and a heavy debt burden, Cracker Barrel is poised to keep losing value for shareholders.”

data.title: T. Rowe Price expands active ETF suite with two equity funds
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) expanded its suite of active exchange-traded fund with two new equity funds, the asset manager said on Thursday.

The new ETFs -- T. Rowe Price Active Core U.S. Equity ETF (TACU) and T. Rowe Price Active Core International Equity ETF (TACN) -- started trading on NYSE Arca on Thursday. Fees will be fully waived for the ETFs first 13 months.

The TACU fund is an actively managed core U.S. large cap portfolio seeking to outperform the broad U.S. equity market with about 550-650 holdings. 

TACN is an actively managed core international equity portfolio that seeks to outperform the global ex-U.S. developed equity markets with ~400-500 holdings.

The launch brings T. Rowe Price's (TROW) roster of active ETFs to 30, including 20 equity ETFs and 10 fixed income offerings.

data.title: Disney alleges Google's AI systems infringed its IP in a cease-and-desist notice
data.description:
Disney (DIS) has sent a cease-and-desist notice to Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and has alleged the tech giant's AI systems have been involved in copyright infringement of its characters on a "massive scale," according to multiple media reports on Thursday.

The report immediately follows the media conglomerate's monumental announcement of a $1B equity investment and a three-year licensing pact with ChatGPT owner OpenAI (OPENAI).

In a letter from law firm Jenner & Block, Disney has accused Google of "copying a large corpus of Disney’s copyrighted works without authorization to train and develop generative artificial intelligence (‘AI’) models and services."

These AI models and services commercially exploit and distribute copies of Disney's protected works to consumers in violation of copyrights, the letter reads.

Disney has asked Google to stop displaying infringed content on its platforms, including through "YouTube’s mobile app, YouTube Shorts, and YouTube," and has asked it to put technological guardrails in its AI services to prevent such copyright violation.

"Here, Google’s conduct is particularly harmful because Google is leveraging its market dominance across multiple channels to distribute its AI Services and using the draw and popularity of infringed copyrighted works to help maintain that dominance," Disney reportedly said in the letter.

Including Google, Disney has sent a similar notice to Meta Platforms (META), but the company has notably filed lawsuits against three AI startups and teamed up with rivals Warner Bros. (WBD) and Universal (CMCSA) in its legal battle to stop IP infringement.

data.title: Cemtrex. announces $2M registered direct offering
data.description:
• Cemtrex (NASDAQ:CETX) announced on Thursday that it has entered into a definitive agreement for the purchase of approximately 0.67M shares with a single institutional investor, at a purchase price of $3 per share, with gross proceeds expected to be $2M.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about Dec 11, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including working capital and potential acquisitions.
  • The offering is being made pursuant to an effective shelf registration statement with the SEC and declared effective on February 3, 2025.

data.title: C3 AI receives FedRAMP authorization, opening the door for government agency adoption
data.description:
C3 AI (AI) has received FedRAMP (Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program) authorization, which opens the door for government agencies to readily adopt its enterprise artificial intelligence software platform, the company announced today.

C3 AI shares ticked up 1% during Thursday morning market action.

"Obtaining FedRAMP authorization underlines that the C3 Agentic AI Platform is trusted, secure and compliant with the stringent standards that federal agencies demand," said C3 AI CEO Stephen Ehikian. "We're proud to deliver to government customers the same Enterprise AI capabilities used by commercial enterprises — now with full federal-grade accreditation."

The authorization has made C3 AI available on the FedRAMP Marketplace.

data.title: Ciena soars on strong Q4 results, guidance
data.description:
Ciena (CIEN) shares jumped more than 5% on Thursday after the networking systems provider reported better-than-expected results and issued strong guidance for the coming quarter and fiscal year. 

For the period ending Nov. 1, Ciena said it earned an adjusted $0.91 per share as revenue rose 20.5% year-over-year to come in at $1.35B. Three customers accounted for 43.6% of total revenue in the quarter, while two customers accounted for 28.4% of revenue for the full-year. Analysts were expecting the company to earn $0.77 per share on $1.29B in revenue.

“Our record fiscal fourth quarter and full-year performance reinforces our position as the global leader in high-speed connectivity with an expanding role in the AI ecosystem,” said Gary Smith, president and CEO, Ciena, in a statement. “Looking ahead, we are confident in our growth trajectory over the coming years, driven by durable demand from our cloud and service provider customers and a growing set of opportunities inside and around the data center.”

Looking to the first-quarter of fiscal 2026, Ciena said it expects to generate between $1.35B and $1.43B in revenue, well above the $1.25B analysts were expecting. Adjusted gross margins are expected to be between 43% and 44%.

For the full-year, sales are forecast to be between $5.7B and $6.1B, also above the $5.53B estimate. Adjusted gross margin is expected to be 43%, plus or minus 1%. The company also said on its earnings call that operating margin is expected to improve and be around 17%, plus or minus 1%. 

Capital expenditures will be between $250M and $275M, aided to support the robust demand and costs for 3 nm mask sets, Ciena added.

The company added it expects to buy back around $330M in stock in fiscal 2026.

(This story has been updated to include conference call information.)

data.title: Nasdaq and S&P 500 fall as Oracle’s post-earnings drop weighs on markets
data.description:
Wall Street trades mixed on Thursday as the tech-oriented Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 drag lower after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) tumbled the hardest as it lost 1.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) pushed down by 0.5% while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed up by 0.4%.

Across sectors, six of the 11 S&P groups are in positive territory led by Materials. At the other end of the spectrum, Info Tech has been the weakest performer so far.

Investors also continue to react to Wednesday’s Fed decision to cut its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3.50%-3.75%, which is now its lowest level in three years. 

“The Federal Reserve sent a clear message that the Fed is in the midst of an accommodative phase, largely driven by the recent weakness in the labor market. While 2026 may not bring as many rate cuts as 2025, it's clear that the Fed's prior hiking cycle that disrupted markets and the economy post-Covid is firmly in the rearview mirror, and that's bullish for stocks,” Chris Kampitsis, managing partner of Barnum Financial Group said. 

Furthermore, Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid stated: “Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning.” Reid noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors. 

Yields were lower across the curve, with the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) falling 3 basis points to 3.51% and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropping 4 basis points to 4.11%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Oracle (ORCL) dropped 14.5% while shares of Mosaic (MOS) pushed up by 5.7%.

data.title: September wholesale inventories grow by more than expected
data.description: Wholesale inventories increased 0.5% M/M to $911.5B in September, stronger than the 0.1% increase expected and August's flat reading, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Thursday.
